title: School Nurse (PreK-8)
subtitle: Student Support Services Division
salaryRange: Salary is competitive and commensurate with experience, structured in accordance with the current collective bargaining agreement metrics.
roleDescription: The School Nurse supports student success by minimizing absence due to illness and maximizing over-all health and wellness. This position delivers direct clinical care, ensures state compliance, and acts as a vital bridge between families, staff, and community health resources.
roleHighlight: Specialized Framework Assignment: This position requires a deep knowledge of the IEP and Section 504 process to effectively assess and support the direct impact of medical factors on student learning configurations.
responsibilities: Provide emergency care with appropriate parent notification and medical referral
Administer medications and treatments and perform specialized health care procedures
Assess individual health needs and develop, implement, and collaborate on student care plans
Ensure compliance with State of Illinois mandates for physical examinations, immunizations, and medical reports
Manage communicable diseases in accordance with Skokie and Illinois Department of Public Health guidelines
Oversee the District 73 Vision and Hearing Screening Program, handling referrals and timely state reporting
Access community resources and develop partnerships to improve health and wellness access for the D73 community
Monitor emerging public health concerns (e.g., infectious diseases) and advise the district on mitigation plans
Implement procedures ensuring compliance with the Illinois School Code and District 73 Board Policies
Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the health office
requiredQuals: Valid Illinois Registered Professional Nurse (RN) License
Deep understanding of the needs and requirements of school health offices
Deep knowledge of the IEP and Section 504 process
Knowledge in pediatrics, public health, mental health, and education frameworks
preferredQuals: Excellent technology skills, including proficiency with Google Suite
Strong interpersonal skills with a demonstrated ability to work collaboratively with colleagues, community, and staff
Demonstrated ability to work independently with minimal supervision while meeting severe time constraints
Maintains professional boundaries and consistently demonstrates ethical behavior
benefits: Comprehensive District-Paid Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance Coverages
Illinois Teachers' Retirement System (TRS) / Professional Pension Participation Framework
Generous Annual Allotments of Paid Sick, Personal, and Family Leave Days
Direct Professional Development Subsidies and Specialized Continuing Education Credits
cultureText: Our health staff operates as an essential pillar of the student services team. We emphasize a collaborative, supportive environment focused on proactive wellness, community resource access, and student safety. Physical demands include regular standing, walking, typing with frequent interruptions, meeting tight deadlines, and occasionally lifting or moving up to 30 pounds.
matchQuestions: Do you hold a current RN license and a valid CPR/AED/First Aid certification?
Are you confident managing Illinois state mandates for immunizations, vision/hearing screenings, and state health reports?
Do you have experience collaborating on IEP/504 teams to assess how medical factors impact student learning?
Can you work independently in a fast-paced environment with frequent interruptions and strict deadlines?
